title: ranunculus paludosus subsp. pseudomillefoliatus
intro: Ranunculus paludosus subsp. pseudomillefoliatus is a subspecies of plant within the buttercup family, scientifically known as Ranunculaceae, and is native to certain parts of the world, including the western United States. This plant is also known as western buttercup or tall buttercup, and it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus paludosus subsp. pseudomillefoliatus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution should be exercised due to the plant's toxic compounds and potential side effects.
